revision.text:
[[de:Ipet-hemet (Monat)]][[ru:Ипет-хемет (месяц)]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs |TITEL = Ipet-hemet |NAME = M17-Q3:X1 U36-t |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Ipet-hemet
''jpt-ḥmt'' |NAME2 = N11:Z1*Z1*Z1*Z1 S-N35B |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION = ''4-nw-šmw'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Fourth month of the Schemu period'' }} '''Ipet-hemet''' referred to the harvest period and the fourth month of the season [[:en:Shemu|Shemu]] in the [[:en:Ancient Egyptian calendar|Ancient Egyptian calendar]]. From the {{ill|Predynastic Egypt|en|Predynastic Egypt|qid=Q714601|lt=Predynastic period|1=Predynastic Egypt}} to the end of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|qid=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom|1=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}}, Ipet-hemet originally represented the twelfth month of the [[Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)#The later lunar calendar|Sothic calendar]], spanning from {{ill|May|en|May|qid=Q119|lt=May 1|1=May}} to May 30. == Background == === Mythology === The {{ill|Myth|en|Myth|qid=Q12827256|lt=mythological background|1=Myth}} was shaped by the hierarchy of deities from the {{ill|Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Fourth Dynasty of Egypt|qid=Q211001|lt=Fourth Dynasty|1=Fourth Dynasty of Egypt}} in the {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|en|Old Kingdom of Egypt|qid=Q177819|lt=Old Kingdom|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt}}, in which [[Ra (Egyptian mythology)|Ra]] as the [[:en:Sun god|Sun god]] held the supreme and first position, merging with other deities. [[Ra-Horakhty]] described Ra as the sun god appearing in the east, the true name of "Ra of {{ill|Heliopolis|en|Heliopolis|qid=Q728863|lt=Heliopolis|1=Heliopolis}}." In {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|en|Thebes, Egypt|qid=Q101583|lt=Thebes|1=Thebes, Egypt}}, {{ill|Amun|en|Amun|qid=Q58373|lt=Amun|1=Amun}} was recorded as a local deity from the {{ill|Eleventh Dynasty of Egypt|en|Eleventh Dynasty of Egypt|qid=Q719634|lt=Eleventh Dynasty|1=Eleventh Dynasty of Egypt}}. To counter the strong Ra worship from the Old Kingdom, the priests merged Amun and Ra under the new name Amun-Ra. Amun was known as "Ra, worshiped in [[:en:Karnak Temple|Karnak]]." In the New Kingdom, he rose as "King of the Gods and Lord of the Thrones of the Two Lands." With the shift to sun worship in the Old Kingdom, the [[:en:Moon god|Moon god]] {{ill|Thoth|en|Thoth|qid=Q146921|lt=Thoth|1=Thoth}} took second place, referred to as "Vizier of Ra," "Scribe of Ra," and "Child of Ra."Tycho Q. Mrsich: ''Fragen zum altägyptischen Recht der "Isolationsperiode" vor dem Neuen Reich – Ein Forschungsbericht aus dem Arbeitskreis "Historiogenese von Rechtsnormen."'' München 2005, § 33. The Sothic calendar reflected this solar worship, with the first month [[Wepet-renpet]] called the "Birth Month of Ra-Horakhty" or "Birth Month of Ra." With the beginning of the {{ill|Nineteenth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Nineteenth Dynasty of Egypt|qid=Q157956|lt=Nineteenth Dynasty|1=Nineteenth Dynasty of Egypt}}, the revival of the old [[Seth (Egyptian mythology)|Seth]] cult occurred, where Seth was depicted as the "Companion of {{ill|Horus|en|Horus|qid=Q84122|lt=Horus|1=Horus}}," shifting the focus to the ancient lunar deity Thoth. === Change in calendar form === In the [[Ebers calendar]] around 1517 BCE, Ipet-hemet was the second Schemu month, dating from May 15 to June 13 ({{ill|Elephantine|en|Elephantine|qid=Q284009|lt=Elephantine|1=Elephantine}}) or from May 20 to June 18 ({{ill|Memphis, Egypt|en|Memphis, Egypt|qid=Q5715|lt=Memphis|1=Memphis, Egypt}}). {{ill|Alan Gardiner|en|Alan Gardiner|qid=Q441060|lt=Alan Gardiner|1=Alan Gardiner}} and {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|en|Richard Anthony Parker|qid=Q2148992|lt=Richard Anthony Parker|1=Richard Anthony Parker}} suggested that Ipet-hemet changed its yearly form throughout calendar history. By the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|qid=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}}, Ipet-hemet had shifted to the eleventh month. The name of the fourth Schemu month later changed to "{{ill|Mesori|en|Mesori|qid=Q1621298|lt=Birth Month of Ra|1=Mesori}}." == References == * [[:en:Rolf Krauss|Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten: Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{False ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}} * Tycho Q. Mrsich: ''Fragen zum altägyptischen Recht der "Isolationsperiode" vor dem Neuen Reich – Ein Forschungsbericht aus dem Arbeitskreis "Historiogenese von Rechtsnormen."'' Utz, München 2005, ISBN 3-89675-970-1. * {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|en|Richard Anthony Parker|qid=Q2148992|lt=Richard Anthony Parker|1=Richard Anthony Parker}}: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt,'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz / Wiesbaden 1950. == Notes == {{Navigation Egyptian Calendar Months before the New Kingdom}} [[ru:%D0%98%D0%BF%D0%B5%D1%82-%D1%85%D0%B5%D0%BC%D0%B5%D1%82 (%D0%BC%D0%B5%D1%81%D1%8F%D1%86)]] [[de:Ipet-hemet (Monat)]] [[fr:%D0%98%D0%BF%D0%B5%D1%82-%D1%85%D0%B5%D0%BC%D0%B5%D1%82 (%D0%BC%D0%B5%D1%81%D1%8F%D1%86)]] [[ru:Ипет-хемет (месяц)]] [[ja:イペット・ヘメット]] {{translated page|de|Ipet-hemet (Monat)|version=250400851|comment=Imported full de history}} {{wikidata link|Q1672177}} [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
